title: salix × capreola
intro: Salix × capreola, also known as the Willow-Gooseberry or Goat Willow, is a hybrid plant species resulting from the cross between Salix caprea and a Salix species, although its exact parentage is uncertain. The medicinal properties of Salix × capreola lie in its bark and leaves, which are rich in salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, making it useful for treating pain and inflammation. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving headaches, and soothing digestive issues. Salix × capreola can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional folk medicine, although its effectiveness and safety may vary depending on the individual and the preparation method.
